storyHook: 指认头手脚，描述感觉与用途

narrative.when: 周二下午，体育课刚结束
narrative.how: 指着不舒服的地方说明情况
narrative.method: 指出疼痛部位、描述感觉、听校医建议、休息观察
narrative.event: 甲跑步时脚扭了一下，担心影响明天的考试

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I have a pain in my foot.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Which part of the foot?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Here, the right foot.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Is your head all right?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, my head is all right.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: And your hands?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Hands are okay, no pain.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: So it is only the foot.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, the right foot is the trouble.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Let me have a look at your foot.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: It is the leg, here.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Is there pain when I put my hand here?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, a sharp pain there.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: No soft growth, that is good.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: So it is a small twist, not broken.
turns.zh: 所以只是轻微扭伤，没骨折。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right, you will be well in a day or two.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: But what about the test tomorrow?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Put a cold cloth on it tonight.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Cold cloth? I will do that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Keep your foot up when you rest.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: May I put weight on it?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Not much. Let it be soft.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: I see. And if it still gives pain?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Come back to me in the morning.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Thank you. I will do that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Now, let me see your foot.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: It is a bit red here.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Is it sharp when I put my hand here?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, that is the bad place.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: It is a small twist, not broken.
turns.zh: 只是轻微扭伤，没伤到骨头。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: What is the name of this part?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is where the leg turns.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: And these are the toes.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: So, head, hands, feet.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: You have them in your head now.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right. What is it like when you put weight on it?
turns.zh: 好的。你踩上去感觉怎么样？

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: It is not sharp, but it is a pull when I do that.
turns.zh: 不是刺痛，但踩的时候有拉扯感。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is normal for a small twist. Rest it for a day.
turns.zh: 轻微扭伤这样正常。休息一天。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Will I be ready for the test tomorrow?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Put a cold cloth on it tonight. If it still gives you pain, come back.
turns.zh: 今晚用冷毛巾敷一下。还疼就再来。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: A cold cloth will make the red part go down.
turns.zh: 冷毛巾能让红肿消下去。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Do I have to keep my foot up?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: A: Yes, put it on a cushion when you are seated.
turns.zh: 对，坐着的时候搁在垫子上。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: How long do I keep the cloth on?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: A: Ten minutes at a time, two or three times.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: No running or jumping till it is better.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: What about walking to the school?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Walk slow. Stop if it gives pain.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: A: I will do that. Thank you.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: You are welcome. be safe of that foot.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: okay then.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Is it up?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: A bit, but not much.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Can you move your toes?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, they move well.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Good. That is a good sign.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Does it give pain when my hand is here?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, that is the sharp point.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: It is a small twist. No broken bones.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: So I can still take the test tomorrow?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: We will see. Let it rest first.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Would you like a cold cloth on it?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Yes, that is a good idea.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: How long do I keep it on?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Ten minutes, then take it off.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Will that make the pain go away?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: It will help. Not all the pain.
turns.zh: 会好一些，但不会完全不疼。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: What about the test in the morning?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: If it still gives you pain, put it off.
turns.zh: 如果还疼，可以申请缓考。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right. I will see how it is in the morning.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: So, what part of the body is this?
turns.zh: 那，这个是身体的哪个部位？

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is the part between the leg and the foot, I would say.
turns.zh: 我觉得那是腿和脚之间的部分。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Right. And this one?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: That is the foot, with toes at the end.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. Now point to your head.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Here. No pain there.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Well, and your hands?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Two hands are all right. See?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: So the trouble is only in the foot.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes. Foot, lower leg, toes. I will keep them in mind.
turns.zh: 对。脚、小腿、脚趾。我会记住的。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Now let me see your foot. Is it red or fat?
turns.zh: 让我看看你的脚。有没有发红或肿起来？

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Not red. But a little fat here, near the bone.
turns.zh: 没红。但骨头旁边这里有点肿。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Is it sharp when I put my finger here?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes! That is the sharp pain I had.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. That is the point. Turn it bit by bit.
turns.zh: 好的。这是那个点。一点一点转。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Oh. Pain when I go this way.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Not too bad. The bone is safe. Only the soft parts.
turns.zh: 不算严重。骨头没事，只是软组织。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: So I can still run tomorrow?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Better not. Rest it with cold cloth tonight.
turns.zh: 最好别跑。今晚用冷毛巾敷一下。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right, leg. I will be safe of you.
turns.zh: 好，腿。我会好好照顾你的。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Let us go over the names again.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Head, hand, foot. I have them now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Which part is damaged?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The part between the foot and the leg.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Good. And the other two?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Head is good. Hands are good.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: So you will keep them safe tomorrow.
turns.zh: 那你明天要保护好它们。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: I will. No running, only rest.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: If it still gives you pain, come back to me.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Thank you. Head, hand, foot. All clear.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Right, what part of the foot is the pain?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: The place where leg bones join on the outer side.
turns.zh: 腿外侧骨头相接的地方。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Is it sharp or not?

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Like a twist, sharp when I put weight on it.
turns.zh: 像扭了一下，踩地就刺痛。

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Let me put my hand here. Is this the place?
turns.zh: 我按一下这里。是这儿吗？

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Yes, that's the worst bit.

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: No growth. It is a small turn.

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Well, so I can still take the test tomorrow?

turns.speaker: A
turns.en: Rest it with a cold cloth tonight. See how it is in the morning.
turns.zh: 今晚冷敷休息，明天早上看情况。

turns.speaker: B
turns.en: Right. I will keep that word in mind.

storyBlurb: 体育课后脚扭了，校医指着头手脚教认部位。边描述疼痛边记名称，担心考试还能不能跑。
storyScript:
【开场·看图指认】周二下午第二节体育课刚结束，甲一瘸一拐地走进保健室。校医乙正坐在桌前，墙上挂着一幅人体部位图，图上的头、手、脚用红笔圈了出来。甲指着自己的右脚踝说，这里不太对劲。乙让他先坐下，指着图上的头部问，头晕吗？甲摇头。乙又指手，甲伸出双手说没事。最后乙指着脚，甲点头说就是这里。乙说，我们先看看脚。

【进行·描述感觉】乙蹲下身，轻轻托起甲的右脚。甲皱眉说，脚踝外侧酸酸胀胀的，像被什么东西扯着。乙问，是刺痛还是钝痛？甲想了想说，像被拧了一下，站着就疼，坐着好一点。乙让他试着慢慢转动脚踝，甲吸了口气说，转到某个角度就疼。乙点点头，用手轻轻按压脚踝周围，甲说就是刚才按的那个点最疼。乙说，没有明显红肿，应该是轻微扭伤，不用太担心。

【进行·听建议记名称】乙让甲把鞋袜脱了，把脚搁在软垫上休息。乙指着甲的脚说，这是脚踝，脚掌，脚趾。甲跟着重复了一遍。乙又说，头、手、脚这三个部位很重要，运动时要保护好。甲问，明天的体育考试怎么办？乙说，今晚回去用冷毛巾敷一敷，明天早上如果还疼就申请缓考。甲松了口气，指着自己的脚踝说，脚踝，记住了。乙笑着指了指墙上的图说，头、手、脚，你都会认了。

【收束·小结记忆】甲休息了十分钟，站起来试了试，脚踝还是有点酸，但比刚才好多了。乙递给他一张纸条，上面写着：头、手、脚。乙说，回家再记一遍，明天如果还疼就来找我。甲点点头，指着自己的头说，头，不晕；又指手，手，没事；最后指脚，脚踝，扭了一下，但我知道它叫什么了。乙笑着挥手，甲慢慢走出保健室，心里默默念着：头、手、脚，脚踝，记住了。
